images.title: Alcyon rouge (Alcyonium glomeratum) (Ifremer 00646-75821 - 30580).jpg
images.description:
Red Alcyon, or "Neptune's fingers", photographed with a diver in Lanvéoc, located on the north coast of the Crozon peninsula and overlooking the harbor of Brest.

This cnidarian is found on rocky bottoms and in poorly lit areas with weak currents in the Atlantic (from the British coast to the Bay of Biscay) and the English Channel. Its numerous polyps, equipped with tentacles with stinging cells, allow it to trap the zooplankton on which it feeds.

The color of the cortex is brick red to dark red, while the polyps are white. The colony size is on average 10 to 15 cm.
